title: kobresia royleana subsp. minshanica
intro: Kobresia royleana subsp. minshanica is a subspecies of sedge, a type of grass-like plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of China and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in the digestion process, due to its rich content of bioactive compounds. Kobresia royleana subsp. minshanica is typically used in herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and decoctions, where its leaves and rhizomes are extracted and consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
